text.title: Claude Model Tier Selection Rules
text.summary: Rules for selecting the right Claude model tier (Haiku, Sonnet, Opus) by task type, setting max_tokens correctly, and avoiding silent truncation of structured output.

rule.statement: Use Haiku for formatting, routing, classification, and simple extraction tasks. Use Sonnet for generation, code, and analysis. Use Opus for complex reasoning, architecture, and strategy tasks.
rule.rationale: Model capability and cost scale together. Using Opus for a simple routing decision wastes ~10x the budget. Using Haiku for complex reasoning produces lower-quality output that may require re-work.

rule.statement: Pin exact model IDs in production configs (e.g., "claude-sonnet-4-20250514") rather than aliases ("claude-sonnet-4").
rule.rationale: Aliases can update to a newer model version without notice. A silent model upgrade can change output format or behavior, breaking downstream parsing.

rule.statement: Log the model actually used in every response, not just the model requested. Log this when implementing fallback logic.
rule.rationale: Fallback to a cheaper model is invisible to the orchestrator unless explicitly logged. Downstream agents may produce worse outputs without knowing the model was downgraded.

rule.statement: Always set max_tokens explicitly on every Messages API call.
rule.rationale: The default max_tokens is low and silently truncates structured output mid-JSON, producing invalid payloads that are hard to debug because the API returns HTTP 200.

rule.statement: When stop_reason == "max_tokens" on a structured output call, the JSON is truncated and invalid. Retry with higher max_tokens; do not parse the response.
rule.rationale: A truncated JSON response from a structured output call will raise a parse error downstream. The correct action is to retry with more headroom, not to handle the parse error.

example.code:
import anthropic
MODELS = { "router": "claude-3-5-haiku-20241022", "generator": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514", "reasoner": "claude-opus-4-5-20251101", }
client = anthropic.Anthropic()
def call(role: str, messages: list, max_tokens: int = 2048) -> str: model = MODELS[role] r = client.messages.create( model=model, max_tokens=max_tokens, messages=messages ) if r.stop_reason == "max_tokens": raise ValueError(f"Response truncated for role={role}. Retry with higher max_tokens.") return r.content[0].text
example.note: max_tokens is explicit and stop_reason is checked before returning the response.
